content.content:
1→"""Text extraction service — first step of the ingestion pipeline.
     2→
     3→Converts all supported formats to plain UTF-8 text before any LLM call.
     4→"""
     5→
     6→from __future__ import annotations
     7→
     8→import logging
     9→from pathlib import Path
    10→
    11→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    12→
    13→# Extension → language label mapping for code files
    14→_CODE_EXTENSIONS: dict[str, str] = {
    15→    ".py": "Python",
    16→    ".sql": "SQL",
    17→    ".js": "JavaScript",
    18→    ".ts": "TypeScript",
    19→    ".jsx": "JSX",
    20→    ".tsx": "TSX",
    21→    ".go": "Go",
    22→    ".rs": "Rust",
    23→    ".java": "Java",
    24→    ".rb": "Ruby",
    25→    ".sh": "Shell",
    26→    ".yaml": "YAML",
    27→    ".yml": "YAML",
    28→    ".toml": "TOML",
    29→    ".json": "JSON",
    30→    ".xml": "XML",
    31→    ".html": "HTML",
    32→    ".css": "CSS",
    33→}
    34→
    35→_TEXT_EXTENSIONS: set[str] = {".txt", ".md", ".markdown", ".rst"}
    36→
    37→
    38→class ExtractionError(Exception):
    39→    """Raised when text extraction fails."""
    40→
    41→    def __init__(self, message: str, source_type: str) -> None:
    42→        self.source_type = source_type
    43→        super().__init__(message)
    44→
    45→
    46→def _ensure_non_empty(text: str, source_type: str, context: str) -> str:
    47→    cleaned = text.strip()
    48→    if not cleaned:
    49→        raise ExtractionError(
    50→            f"Extraction yielded empty text from {context}", source_type
    51→        )
    52→    return cleaned
    53→
    54→
    55→def _extract_pdf(file_path: str) -> str:
    56→    """Extract text from a PDF, falling back to OCR if needed."""
    57→    import pdfplumber
    58→
    59→    pages: list[str] = []
    60→    with pdfplumber.open(file_path) as pdf:
    61→        for page in pdf.pages:
    62→            page_text = page.extract_text() or ""
    63→            pages.append(page_text)
    64→
    65→    combined = "\n\n--- Page Break ---\n\n".join(pages).strip()
    66→
    67→    if len(combined.replace("--- Page Break ---", "").strip()) < 10:
    68→        logger.warning(
    69→            "pdfplumber returned near-empty text for %s — attempting OCR fallback",
    70→            file_path,
    71→        )
    72→        combined = _extract_pdf_ocr(file_path)
    73→
    74→    return combined
    75→
    76→
    77→def _extract_pdf_ocr(file_path: str) -> str:
    78→    """OCR fallback for scanned PDFs."""
    79→    import pdf2image
    80→    import pytesseract
    81→
    82→    images = pdf2image.convert_from_path(file_path)
    83→    pages: list[str] = []
    84→    for img in images:
    85→        text = pytesseract.image_to_string(img)
    86→        pages.append(text)
    87→
    88→    return "\n\n--- Page Break ---\n\n".join(pages).strip()
    89→
    90→
    91→def _extract_docx(file_path: str) -> str:
    92→    """Extract text from a DOCX file."""
    93→    from docx import Document
    94→
    95→    doc = Document(file_path)
    96→    return "\n".join(p.text for p in doc.paragraphs)
    97→
    98→
    99→def _extract_url(url: str) -> str:
   100→    """Fetch and extract main content from a URL."""
   101→    import trafilatura
   102→
   103→    downloaded = trafilatura.fetch_url(url)
   104→    if downloaded is None:
   105→        raise ExtractionError(
   106→            f"Failed to fetch URL: {url}", source_type="url"
   107→        )
   108→
   109→    text = trafilatura.extract(downloaded)
   110→    if text is None:
   111→        raise ExtractionError(
   112→            f"Failed to extract content from URL: {url}", source_type="url"
   113→        )
   114→    return text
   115→
   116→
   117→def _read_file(file_path: str) -> str:
   118→    return Path(file_path).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
   119→
   120→
   121→async def extract_text(
   122→    file_path: str | None = None,
   123→    url: str | None = None,
   124→    raw_text: str | None = None,
   125→    source_type: str = "",
   126→) -> str:
   127→    """Extract plain UTF-8 text from a supported source.
   128→
   129→    Exactly one of *file_path*, *url*, or *raw_text* should be provided.
   130→
   131→    Returns clean UTF-8 text ready for downstream processing.
   132→    Raises ``ExtractionError`` on any failure.
   133→    """
   134→    try:
   135→        if raw_text is not None:
   136→            return _ensure_non_empty(raw_text, source_type, "raw text input")
   137→
   138→        if url is not None:
   139→            text = _extract_url(url)
   140→            return _ensure_non_empty(text, source_type, url)
   141→
   142→        if file_path is None:
   143→            raise ExtractionError(
   144→                "No input provided — supply file_path, url, or raw_text",
   145→                source_type,
   146→            )
   147→
   148→        ext = Path(file_path).suffix.lower()
   149→
   150→        if ext == ".pdf":
   151→            text = _extract_pdf(file_path)
   152→            return _ensure_non_empty(text, source_type, file_path)
   153→
   154→        if ext == ".docx":
   155→            text = _extract_docx(file_path)
   156→            return _ensure_non_empty(text, source_type, file_path)
   157→
   158→        if ext in _CODE_EXTENSIONS:
   159→            content = _read_file(file_path)
   160→            lang = _CODE_EXTENSIONS[ext]
   161→            text = f"# Language: {lang}\n{content}"
   162→            return _ensure_non_empty(text, source_type, file_path)
   163→
   164→        if ext in _TEXT_EXTENSIONS:
   165→            text = _read_file(file_path)
   166→            return _ensure_non_empty(text, source_type, file_path)
   167→
   168→        raise ExtractionError(
   169→            f"Unsupported file type: {ext}", source_type
   170→        )
   171→
   172→    except ExtractionError:
   173→        raise
   174→    except Exception as exc:
   175→        raise ExtractionError(
   176→            f"Extraction failed: {exc}", source_type
   177→        ) from exc
   178→

1→"""Tests for the text extraction service."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→from pathlib import Path
     6→from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
     7→
     8→import pytest
     9→
    10→from my_note.services.extractor import ExtractionError, extract_text
    11→
    12→
    13→# ── Raw text ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    14→
    15→
    16→@pytest.mark.asyncio
    17→async def test_raw_text():
    18→    result = await extract_text(raw_text="Hello, world!", source_type="text")
    19→    assert result == "Hello, world!"
    20→
    21→
    22→@pytest.mark.asyncio
    23→async def test_raw_text_empty_raises():
    24→    with pytest.raises(ExtractionError):
    25→        await extract_text(raw_text="   ", source_type="text")
    26→
    27→
    28→# ── Text / Markdown files ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
    29→
    30→
    31→@pytest.mark.asyncio
    32→async def test_markdown_file(tmp_path: Path):
    33→    md = tmp_path / "note.md"
    34→    md.write_text("# Heading\n\nSome content.", encoding="utf-8")
    35→    result = await extract_text(file_path=str(md), source_type="markdown")
    36→    assert "# Heading" in result
    37→    assert "Some content." in result
    38→
    39→
    40→@pytest.mark.asyncio
    41→async def test_txt_file(tmp_path: Path):
    42→    txt = tmp_path / "note.txt"
    43→    txt.write_text("Plain text content.", encoding="utf-8")
    44→    result = await extract_text(file_path=str(txt), source_type="text")
    45→    assert result == "Plain text content."
    46→
    47→
    48→# ── Code files ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    49→
    50→
    51→@pytest.mark.asyncio
    52→async def test_python_file(tmp_path: Path):
    53→    py = tmp_path / "script.py"
    54→    py.write_text("print('hi')\n", encoding="utf-8")
    55→    result = await extract_text(file_path=str(py), source_type="code")
    56→    assert result.startswith("# Language: Python")
    57→    assert "print('hi')" in result
    58→
    59→
    60→@pytest.mark.asyncio
    61→async def test_typescript_file(tmp_path: Path):
    62→    ts = tmp_path / "app.ts"
    63→    ts.write_text("const x: number = 1;\n", encoding="utf-8")
    64→    result = await extract_text(file_path=str(ts), source_type="code")
    65→    assert result.startswith("# Language: TypeScript")
    66→
    67→
    68→@pytest.mark.asyncio
    69→async def test_json_file(tmp_path: Path):
    70→    f = tmp_path / "data.json"
    71→    f.write_text('{"key": "value"}', encoding="utf-8")
    72→    result = await extract_text(file_path=str(f), source_type="code")
    73→    assert "# Language: JSON" in result
    74→
    75→
    76→# ── PDF extraction ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    77→
    78→
    79→@pytest.mark.asyncio
    80→async def test_pdf_extraction(tmp_path: Path):
    81→    fake_pdf = tmp_path / "doc.pdf"
    82→    fake_pdf.touch()
    83→
    84→    mock_page = MagicMock()
    85→    mock_page.extract_text.return_value = "Page one content."
    86→
    87→    mock_pdf_ctx = MagicMock()
    88→    mock_pdf_ctx.pages = [mock_page]
    89→    mock_pdf_ctx.__enter__ = MagicMock(return_value=mock_pdf_ctx)
    90→    mock_pdf_ctx.__exit__ = MagicMock(return_value=False)
    91→
    92→    with patch.dict("sys.modules", {"pdfplumber": MagicMock()}) as _:
    93→        import sys
    94→
    95→        sys.modules["pdfplumber"].open.return_value = mock_pdf_ctx
    96→        result = await extract_text(file_path=str(fake_pdf), source_type="pdf")
    97→
    98→    assert "Page one content." in result
    99→
   100→
   101→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   102→async def test_pdf_multi_page(tmp_path: Path):
   103→    fake_pdf = tmp_path / "doc.pdf"
   104→    fake_pdf.touch()
   105→
   106→    pages = []
   107→    for text in ["First page.", "Second page."]:
   108→        p = MagicMock()
   109→        p.extract_text.return_value = text
   110→        pages.append(p)
   111→
   112→    mock_pdf_ctx = MagicMock()
   113→    mock_pdf_ctx.pages = pages
   114→    mock_pdf_ctx.__enter__ = MagicMock(return_value=mock_pdf_ctx)
   115→    mock_pdf_ctx.__exit__ = MagicMock(return_value=False)
   116→
   117→    with patch.dict("sys.modules", {"pdfplumber": MagicMock()}):
   118→        import sys
   119→
   120→        sys.modules["pdfplumber"].open.return_value = mock_pdf_ctx
   121→        result = await extract_text(file_path=str(fake_pdf), source_type="pdf")
   122→
   123→    assert "First page." in result
   124→    assert "Second page." in result
   125→    assert "--- Page Break ---" in result
   126→
   127→
   128→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   129→async def test_pdf_ocr_fallback(tmp_path: Path):
   130→    """OCR fallback triggers when pdfplumber returns empty text."""
   131→    fake_pdf = tmp_path / "scanned.pdf"
   132→    fake_pdf.touch()
   133→
   134→    mock_page = MagicMock()
   135→    mock_page.extract_text.return_value = ""
   136→
   137→    mock_pdf_ctx = MagicMock()
   138→    mock_pdf_ctx.pages = [mock_page]
   139→    mock_pdf_ctx.__enter__ = MagicMock(return_value=mock_pdf_ctx)
   140→    mock_pdf_ctx.__exit__ = MagicMock(return_value=False)
   141→
   142→    mock_img = MagicMock()
   143→
   144→    mock_pdfplumber = MagicMock()
   145→    mock_pdfplumber.open.return_value = mock_pdf_ctx
   146→
   147→    mock_pdf2image = MagicMock()
   148→    mock_pdf2image.convert_from_path.return_value = [mock_img]
   149→
   150→    mock_tesseract = MagicMock()
   151→    mock_tesseract.image_to_string.return_value = "OCR extracted text"
   152→
   153→    with patch.dict(
   154→        "sys.modules",
   155→        {
   156→            "pdfplumber": mock_pdfplumber,
   157→            "pdf2image": mock_pdf2image,
   158→            "pytesseract": mock_tesseract,
   159→        },
   160→    ):
   161→        result = await extract_text(
   162→            file_path=str(fake_pdf), source_type="pdf"
   163→        )
   164→
   165→    assert "OCR extracted text" in result
   166→
   167→
   168→# ── DOCX extraction ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   169→
   170→
   171→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   172→async def test_docx_extraction(tmp_path: Path):
   173→    fake_docx = tmp_path / "doc.docx"
   174→    fake_docx.touch()
   175→
   176→    mock_para1 = MagicMock()
   177→    mock_para1.text = "First paragraph."
   178→    mock_para2 = MagicMock()
   179→    mock_para2.text = "Second paragraph."
   180→
   181→    mock_doc = MagicMock()
   182→    mock_doc.paragraphs = [mock_para1, mock_para2]
   183→
   184→    mock_docx_module = MagicMock()
   185→    mock_docx_module.Document.return_value = mock_doc
   186→
   187→    with patch.dict("sys.modules", {"docx": mock_docx_module}):
   188→        result = await extract_text(
   189→            file_path=str(fake_docx), source_type="docx"
   190→        )
   191→
   192→    assert "First paragraph." in result
   193→    assert "Second paragraph." in result
   194→
   195→
   196→# ── URL extraction ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   197→
   198→
   199→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   200→async def test_url_extraction():
   201→    mock_traf = MagicMock()
   202→    mock_traf.fetch_url.return_value = "<html>content</html>"
   203→    mock_traf.extract.return_value = "Extracted article text."
   204→
   205→    with patch.dict("sys.modules", {"trafilatura": mock_traf}):
   206→        result = await extract_text(
   207→            url="https://example.com/article", source_type="url"
   208→        )
   209→
   210→    assert result == "Extracted article text."
   211→
   212→
   213→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   214→async def test_url_fetch_failure():
   215→    mock_traf = MagicMock()
   216→    mock_traf.fetch_url.return_value = None
   217→
   218→    with patch.dict("sys.modules", {"trafilatura": mock_traf}):
   219→        with pytest.raises(ExtractionError, match="Failed to fetch URL"):
   220→            await extract_text(
   221→                url="https://example.com/bad", source_type="url"
   222→            )
   223→
   224→
   225→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   226→async def test_url_extract_failure():
   227→    mock_traf = MagicMock()
   228→    mock_traf.fetch_url.return_value = "<html></html>"
   229→    mock_traf.extract.return_value = None
   230→
   231→    with patch.dict("sys.modules", {"trafilatura": mock_traf}):
   232→        with pytest.raises(ExtractionError, match="Failed to extract content"):
   233→            await extract_text(
   234→                url="https://example.com/empty", source_type="url"
   235→            )
   236→
   237→
   238→# ── Error handling ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   239→
   240→
   241→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   242→async def test_unsupported_file_type(tmp_path: Path):
   243→    f = tmp_path / "image.bmp"
   244→    f.touch()
   245→    with pytest.raises(ExtractionError, match="Unsupported file type"):
   246→        await extract_text(file_path=str(f), source_type="unknown")
   247→
   248→
   249→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   250→async def test_no_input_raises():
   251→    with pytest.raises(ExtractionError, match="No input provided"):
   252→        await extract_text(source_type="none")
   253→
   254→
   255→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   256→async def test_extraction_error_has_source_type():
   257→    with pytest.raises(ExtractionError) as exc_info:
   258→        await extract_text(raw_text="", source_type="text")
   259→    assert exc_info.value.source_type == "text"
   260→

file.content:
"""Tests for the text extraction service."""

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

import pytest

from my_note.services.extractor import ExtractionError, extract_text


# ── Raw text ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_raw_text():
    result = await extract_text(raw_text="Hello, world!", source_type="text")
    assert result == "Hello, world!"


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_raw_text_empty_raises():
    with pytest.raises(ExtractionError):
        await extract_text(raw_text="   ", source_type="text")


# ── Text / Markdown files ──────────────────────────────────────────────────


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_markdown_file(tmp_path: Path):
    md = tmp_path / "note.md"
    md.write_text("# Heading\n\nSome content.", encoding="utf-8")
    result = await extract_text(file_path=str(md), source_type="markdown")
    assert "# Heading" in result
    assert "Some content." in result


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_txt_file(tmp_path: Path):
    txt = tmp_path / "note.txt"
    txt.write_text("Plain text content.", encoding="utf-8")
    result = await extract_text(file_path=str(txt), source_type="text")
    assert result == "Plain text content."


# ── Code files ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_python_file(tmp_path: Path):
    py = tmp_path / "script.py"
    py.write_text("print('hi')\n", encoding="utf-8")
    result = await extract_text(file_path=str(py), source_type="code")
    assert result.startswith("# Language: Python")
    assert "print('hi')" in result


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_typescript_file(tmp_path: Path):
    ts = tmp_path / "app.ts"
    ts.write_text("const x: number = 1;\n", encoding="utf-8")
    result = await extract_text(file_path=str(ts), source_type="code")
    assert result.startswith("# Language: TypeScript")


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_json_file(tmp_path: Path):
    f = tmp_path / "data.json"
    f.write_text('{"key": "value"}', encoding="utf-8")
    result = await extract_text(file_path=str(f), source_type="code")
    assert "# Language: JSON" in result


# ── PDF extraction ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_pdf_extraction(tmp_path: Path):
    fake_pdf = tmp_path / "doc.pdf"
    fake_pdf.touch()

    mock_page = MagicMock()
    mock_page.extract_text.return_value = "Page one content."

    mock_pdf_ctx = MagicMock()
    mock_pdf_ctx.pages = [mock_page]
    mock_pdf_ctx.__enter__ = MagicMock(return_value=mock_pdf_ctx)
    mock_pdf_ctx.__exit__ = MagicMock(return_value=False)

    with patch.dict("sys.modules", {"pdfplumber": MagicMock()}) as _:
        import sys

        sys.modules["pdfplumber"].open.return_value = mock_pdf_ctx
        result = await extract_text(file_path=str(fake_pdf), source_type="pdf")

    assert "Page one content." in result


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_pdf_multi_page(tmp_path: Path):
    fake_pdf = tmp_path / "doc.pdf"
    fake_pdf.touch()

    pages = []
    for text in ["First page.", "Second page."]:
        p = MagicMock()
        p.extract_text.return_value = text
        pages.append(p)

    mock_pdf_ctx = MagicMock()
    mock_pdf_ctx.pages = pages
    mock_pdf_ctx.__enter__ = MagicMock(return_value=mock_pdf_ctx)
    mock_pdf_ctx.__exit__ = MagicMock(return_value=False)

    with patch.dict("sys.modules", {"pdfplumber": MagicMock()}):
        import sys

        sys.modules["pdfplumber"].open.return_value = mock_pdf_ctx
        result = await extract_text(file_path=str(fake_pdf), source_type="pdf")

    assert "First page." in result
    assert "Second page." in result
    assert "--- Page Break ---" in result


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_pdf_ocr_fallback(tmp_path: Path):
    """OCR fallback triggers when pdfplumber returns empty text."""
    fake_pdf = tmp_path / "scanned.pdf"
    fake_pdf.touch()

    mock_page = MagicMock()
    mock_page.extract_text.return_value = ""

    mock_pdf_ctx = MagicMock()
    mock_pdf_ctx.pages = [mock_page]
    mock_pdf_ctx.__enter__ = MagicMock(return_value=mock_pdf_ctx)
    mock_pdf_ctx.__exit__ = MagicMock(return_value=False)

    mock_img = MagicMock()

    mock_pdfplumber = MagicMock()
    mock_pdfplumber.open.return_value = mock_pdf_ctx

    mock_pdf2image = MagicMock()
    mock_pdf2image.convert_from_path.return_value = [mock_img]

    mock_tesseract = MagicMock()
    mock_tesseract.image_to_string.return_value = "OCR extracted text"

    with patch.dict(
        "sys.modules",
        {
            "pdfplumber": mock_pdfplumber,
            "pdf2image": mock_pdf2image,
            "pytesseract": mock_tesseract,
        },
    ):
        result = await extract_text(
            file_path=str(fake_pdf), source_type="pdf"
        )

    assert "OCR extracted text" in result


# ── DOCX extraction ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_docx_extraction(tmp_path: Path):
    fake_docx = tmp_path / "doc.docx"
    fake_docx.touch()

    mock_para1 = MagicMock()
    mock_para1.text = "First paragraph."
    mock_para2 = MagicMock()
    mock_para2.text = "Second paragraph."

    mock_doc = MagicMock()
    mock_doc.paragraphs = [mock_para1, mock_para2]

    mock_docx_module = MagicMock()
    mock_docx_module.Document.return_value = mock_doc

    with patch.dict("sys.modules", {"docx": mock_docx_module}):
        result = await extract_text(
            file_path=str(fake_docx), source_type="docx"
        )

    assert "First paragraph." in result
    assert "Second paragraph." in result


# ── URL extraction ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_url_extraction():
    mock_traf = MagicMock()
    mock_traf.fetch_url.return_value = "<html>content</html>"
    mock_traf.extract.return_value = "Extracted article text."

    with patch.dict("sys.modules", {"trafilatura": mock_traf}):
        result = await extract_text(
            url="https://example.com/article", source_type="url"
        )

    assert result == "Extracted article text."


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_url_fetch_failure():
    mock_traf = MagicMock()
    mock_traf.fetch_url.return_value = None

    with patch.dict("sys.modules", {"trafilatura": mock_traf}):
        with pytest.raises(ExtractionError, match="Failed to fetch URL"):
            await extract_text(
                url="https://example.com/bad", source_type="url"
            )


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_url_extract_failure():
    mock_traf = MagicMock()
    mock_traf.fetch_url.return_value = "<html></html>"
    mock_traf.extract.return_value = None

    with patch.dict("sys.modules", {"trafilatura": mock_traf}):
        with pytest.raises(ExtractionError, match="Failed to extract content"):
            await extract_text(
                url="https://example.com/empty", source_type="url"
            )


# ── Error handling ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_unsupported_file_type(tmp_path: Path):
    f = tmp_path / "image.bmp"
    f.touch()
    with pytest.raises(ExtractionError, match="Unsupported file type"):
        await extract_text(file_path=str(f), source_type="unknown")


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_no_input_raises():
    with pytest.raises(ExtractionError, match="No input provided"):
        await extract_text(source_type="none")


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_extraction_error_has_source_type():
    with pytest.raises(ExtractionError) as exc_info:
        await extract_text(raw_text="", source_type="text")
    assert exc_info.value.source_type == "text"

[project]
name = "my-note"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Local-first AI knowledge agent"
requires-python = ">=3.12"
dependencies = [
    "fastapi>=0.115",
    "uvicorn[standard]>=0.34",
    "pydantic-settings>=2.7",
    "aiosqlite>=0.21",
    "httpx>=0.28",
    "pdfplumber>=0.11",
    "pdf2image>=1.17",
    "pytesseract>=0.3",
    "python-docx>=1.1",
    "trafilatura>=1.12",
]

[build-system]
requires = ["hatchling"]
build-backend = "hatchling.build"

[project.optional-dependencies]
test = [
    "pytest>=8.0",
    "pytest-asyncio>=0.24",
]

[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
packages = ["src/my_note"]

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
asyncio_mode = "auto"
